thinking: The user wants me to launch three agents in parallel to gather information, then compare the results.

tool_uses.name: Agent
tool_uses.input:
{"description": "Search Anthropic API pricing", "subagent_type": "general-purpose", "prompt": "Search the web for the latest Anthropic API pricing for all Claude models (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) as of April 2026. I need:
- Input token cost per million tokens
- Output token cost per million tokens
- Cache read and cache write token costs if available
- Pricing for all model tiers: Claude Opus 4/4.6, Claude Sonnet 4/4.5/4.6, Claude Haiku 3.5/4.5

Report the pricing in a clear table format. This is a research-only task - do not write any code."}

tool_uses.name: Agent
tool_uses.input: {"description": "Read usage.rs pricing constants", "subagent_type": "Explore", "prompt": "Read the file rust/crates/runtime/src/usage.rs and extract all pricing constants defined in the code. I need to see every cost/price constant, what model it maps to, and the values for input tokens, output tokens, cache read tokens, and cache write tokens. Report everything you find - this is research only, no code changes needed. Be thorough - check for any related pricing files nearby too."}

tool_uses.name: Agent
tool_uses.input:
{"description": "Read types.rs cost calculations", "subagent_type": "Explore", "prompt": "Read the file rust/crates/api/src/types.rs and look for how costs are calculated. I need to understand:
1. What cost/pricing structs or types are defined
2. How token costs are computed (any formulas or methods)
3. What models are referenced in cost calculations
4. Any cost-related constants or default values

Report everything you find about cost/pricing logic. This is research only, no code changes."}
